summary: Typosquat of libsignal-node patches @whiskeysockets/baileys to hijack installer's WhatsApp session
details: Package name and description impersonate Open Whisper Systems' legitimate libsignal-node library. On require, index.js schedules install.js (via setTimeout 1000ms) which locates the installer's @whiskeysockets/baileys package on disk and overwrites lib/Socket/newsletter.js with attacker-controlled JavaScript. The injected code sets a 120-second timer that fetches a mutable channel-ID list from https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pipih071/SilenceV3/refs/heads/main/ch.json and calls newsletterWMexQuery(id, QueryIds.FOLLOW) for each entry, abusing the host bot's authenticated WhatsApp session to silently subscribe the installer's WhatsApp account to attacker-chosen newsletters with no consent. A.cache marker is written to avoid re-patching. This combines three independently sufficient attack patterns: (1) typosquat impersonating libsignal-node, (2) tampering with an unrelated installed package's source files on disk, and (3) silent-relay of the installer's authenticated WhatsApp session to perform attacker-directed actions driven by a mutable personal-GitHub URL.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
